35 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:豊日別 up to rev 98795603
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2021年8月}} {{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 =豊日別 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = 250px | 画像説明 = | 世代名 = | 先代 = | 次代 = | 神祇 = | 全名 = | 別名 = [[豊国]] | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = [[国魂]] | 陵所 = <!-- 日向三代用 --> | 父 = [[伊邪那岐命]] | 母 = [[伊邪那美命]] | 親 = | 配偶者 = | 子 = | 宮 = | 神社 = [[豊日別宮]] | 御利益 = | 記紀 = | 関連氏族 = }} '''豊日別'''（とよひわけ）は『[[古事記]]』に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』[[国産み]]の段において、[[伊邪那岐命]]・[[伊邪那美命]]が産んだ[[八島|大八島国]]の一つである[[九州|筑紫島]]は、[[伊予之二名島]]と同じく体は1つだが顔は4つあるとした。その2番目が[[豊国]]であるが、神の顔の名は豊日別であると記している。他の3つの顔は[[筑紫国]]の[[白日別]]、[[肥国]]の[[建日向日豊久士比泥別]]、[[熊襲国]]の[[建日別]]である。
{{quote|次生筑紫嶋、此嶋亦、身一而有面四、毎面有名、故、筑紫國謂白日別、'''豐國謂豐日別'''、肥國謂建日向日豐久士比泥別自久至泥、以音、熊曾國謂建日別。曾字以音。|古事記}}
ただし、江戸中期の神道学者の[[出口延佳|度会延佳]]は、筑紫国や豊国といった国名は、いささか後世になってからの名称であって、[[神世七代|神代]]には、国名が白日別、豊日別などの名称だったのであろう、筑紫島が9国に分かれ[[西海道]]と呼ばれるようになったのも（[[律令制]]が敷かれた）のちのことである、としている{{sfn|佐藤蔵太郎|1905}}。
== 祀る神社 == [[到津八幡神社]]、[[豊日別宮]]（草場神社）、[[闇無浜神社]]、[[菅原神社 (北九州市小倉北区)|菅原神社]]、[[高住神社]]、[[温泉神社 (雲仙市)|温泉神社]]などに祀られている。
;豊日別宮 [[豊日別宮]]（[[福岡県]][[行橋市]]）の社伝では、6世紀の[[欽明天皇|欽明]]天皇2年、[[筑紫国]]の日別大神の神官・大伴連牟彌奈里に「吾は[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦神]]なり」と神託があり、猿田彦は[[天照大神]]の分神であり、 豊日別大神を本宮とし、猿田彦を別宮とするとの記述がある。ここから、豊日別は猿田彦と同一神格と見られることもあるが、[[道祖神]]とはやや異なろう。
豊日別宮の社伝は、豊日別が[[高御産巣日神]]と入れ替わったように見えるが、両者が同一の神格であることを示す傍証とも言えよう。或いは「豊日別」が高木神の称号または役職名であったのかもしれない。初期王朝に於ける「別」「耳」は、首長または国の名に使われている。
;温泉神社 [[高来郡]]（[[肥前国]]）ではこの四面神を「お四面さま」として祀り、[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮] と称した。[[温泉神社 (雲仙市)|雲仙]]を本宮とし、山田・千々石・有江（有家）・伊佐早（諫早）に分霊を置いて遥拝した。後、島原半島一円に広がった。キリシタン大名有馬晴信により、その家臣団が煽動した島原の乱により、多くが破壊されてなお存続したが、開化期に至り「温泉（うんぜん）神社」と改称した。[[長崎県]][[雲仙市]][[小浜町 (長崎県)|小浜町]]、別名「四面宮」。
;高住神社 高住神社は、[[鷹巣山]]を拝する鷹栖宮であるが、豊日別を[[英彦山]]北岳の高住神社に祀る。八大天狗の彦山豊前坊としても知られ、北部九州に散在する「豊前坊」「豊日社」は、この神を英彦山修験が祀ったものである。
;高木神社（英彦山） 英彦山麓に林立する高木神社は、元は大行事社といった。この大行事権現は[[タカミムスビ|高御産巣日神]]であり、猿田彦大神に変身して[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]]の天下りを助けたと伝わる。 {{quote|大行事権現は。猿田彦神にて御す也。又は衢靈神とも號す。此の猿田彦と申すは。十禅師権現母方の祖父御前。高皇産靈命皇孫降臨の時。三百六十種の御寳物を譲り玉ひけるも。此の國には地祇部類邪悪の神達多く御す故に。路次の間何にも心苦く思し召し。鬼王の如くなる威徳無双の神躰に化し。面を赤く鼻長く。左右の目は日月の如く耀。おそろしげなる形にて。鉾つきて。十禅師権現の天降り玉ふ路次に先立ちて。異相斯くの如き人と御す由告げ来たれば。怖れ畏み有る処に。天鈿女命を出だし。我行きて其の子細を尋ねんとて。彼の在所に至りて。元は何なる人と問ひ玉ふに。答て云ふ。我をば猿田彦と號す。實には高皇産靈命也。皇孫降臨路次を守護の為。此の鉾を以て邪鬼の者を拂ひ退け。諸道の印を結びて道路を鎭する故に。我が跡に付て神幸有らば。もろもろの怖れ有るべからずと畏み示し玉ふ。鈿女命此の事を奏し玉ふに。諸神大きに悦びて。成無異無事降臨玉ふ。大行事権現は。猿田彦の神にて御す故に。此の鎭道の神の御形状傳か。今の王舞有之。此の王の舞と師子との不通を神幸無事也。大行事は十禅師を擁護す。今の社稷も十禅師に向かひ玉ふ。凡そ山王の惣後見は。一切の行事をば。此の大行事権現の成し玉ふ事也。山王勧請の所には。何くにも又此の神を奉斎すへき事也。されば鹽下の惣社にも。惠心先德自ら猿の行事の神躰を奉造り崇め制玉へり是を彌行事と號く。|『続群書類従』}}
古事記は、『是高木神者、高御産巣日神之別名。』として、天下り事業を指揮する途中、[[天若日子]]を処断する時に神名を変える。対して日本書紀では「[[たかみむすび|高皇産靈命]]」で通すのだが、この名を冠する神社は北部九州では見かけず、「高木」「鷹栖」ばかり。十禅寺とは[[国常立尊]]から数えて10代目の意で、[[瓊瓊杵尊]]（ににぎ）を指す。[[英彦山]]は瓊瓊杵尊の父、[[アメノオシホミミ|天之忍穂耳命]]（あめのおしほみみ）の鎮まる山であり、瓊瓊杵尊を祀る社殿もある。
;雲仙四面宮 雲仙四面宮も、筑紫[[国魂]]社を名乗ったことがある。[[雲仙岳]]は高来山とも呼ばれ、筑後平野から仰ぎ見る[[神奈備]]であった。
四面宮改め温泉神社群については、最近になって [https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会のホームページ] が出来たので、そちらを参照されたい。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} ;注釈 {{notelist}} ;出典 {{reflist}}
==関連項目== *[[国産み]] *[[日本の神の一覧]] *[[豊国]] *[[前方後円墳]]
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book| 和書| editor=| translator=| author=佐藤蔵太郎|authorlink=佐藤蔵太郎| year=1905| title=[{{NDLDC|766779/18}} 豊後史蹟考]| publisher=[[甲斐書店]]| location =大分県| page=|section=| quote=| isbn=| ref=harv}}
== 外部リンク ==
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよひわけ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:豊国の人物]]

;温泉神社 [[高来郡]]（[[肥前国]]）ではこの四面神を「お四面さま」として祀り、[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮] と称した。[[温泉神社 (雲仙市)|雲仙]]を本宮とし、山田・千々石・有江（有家）・伊佐早（諫早）に分霊を置いて遥拝した。後、島原半島一円に広がった。キリシタン大名有馬晴信により、その家臣団が煽動した島原の乱により、多くが破壊されてなお存続したが、開化期に至り「温泉（うんぜん）神社」と改称した。[[長崎県]][[雲仙市]][[小浜町 (長崎県)|小浜町]]、別名「四面宮」。

;高木神社（英彦山） 英彦山麓に林立する高木神社は、元は大行事社といった。この大行事権現は[[タカミムスビ|高御産巣日神]]であり、猿田彦大神に変身して[[ニニギ|瓊瓊杵尊]]の天下りを助けたと伝わる。 {{quote|大行事権現は。猿田彦神にて御す也。又は衢靈神とも號す。此の猿田彦と申すは。十禅師権現母方の祖父御前。高皇産靈命皇孫降臨の時。三百六十種の御寳物を譲り玉ひけるも。此の國には地祇部類邪悪の神達多く御す故に。路次の間何にも心苦く思し召し。鬼王の如くなる威徳無双の神躰に化し。面を赤く鼻長く。左右の目は日月の如く耀。おそろしげなる形にて。鉾つきて。十禅師権現の天降り玉ふ路次に先立ちて。異相斯くの如き人と御す由告げ来たれば。怖れ畏み有る処に。天鈿女命を出だし。我行きて其の子細を尋ねんとて。彼の在所に至りて。元は何なる人と問ひ玉ふに。答て云ふ。我をば猿田彦と號す。實には高皇産靈命也。皇孫降臨路次を守護の為。此の鉾を以て邪鬼の者を拂ひ退け。諸道の印を結びて道路を鎭する故に。我が跡に付て神幸有らば。もろもろの怖れ有るべからずと畏み示し玉ふ。鈿女命此の事を奏し玉ふに。諸神大きに悦びて。成無異無事降臨玉ふ。大行事権現は。猿田彦の神にて御す故に。此の鎭道の神の御形状傳か。今の王舞有之。此の王の舞と師子との不通を神幸無事也。大行事は十禅師を擁護す。今の社稷も十禅師に向かひ玉ふ。凡そ山王の惣後見は。一切の行事をば。此の大行事権現の成し玉ふ事也。山王勧請の所には。何くにも又此の神を奉斎すへき事也。されば鹽下の惣社にも。惠心先德自ら猿の行事の神躰を奉造り崇め制玉へり是を彌行事と號く。|『続群書類従』}}

== 外部リンク ==
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよひわけ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:豊国の人物]]

{{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Toyohiwake | image = | caption = | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | classification = [[Kunitama]] (Spirit of the Land) | full_name = | alternate_name = {{ill|豊国|ja}}|other_names = | Rank = | role = Spirit of the land ([[Kunitama]]) | burial_site= | father = [[Izanagi]] | mother = [[Izanami]] | siblings = | spouse = | children = | shrines = [[Toyohiwake-no-miya]] and others | blessings = | myths = | related_clans =|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Toyohiwake''' ({{lang|ja|豊日別}}) is a deity featured in the [[Kojiki]] and is associated with the creation of Japan's lands.
== Overview == In the section of the [[Kojiki]] describing the [[Kuniumi|creation of the land]], the deities [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] gave birth to the great islands of Japan, known as the [[Ōyashima]]. The island of [[Kyushu]], like [[Iyo-no-futana-shima]] (modern [[Shikoku]]), is said to have one body but four faces, each with its own name. The second face, corresponding to the land of [[Toyokuni]] (modern [[Bungo Province]]), is called Toyohiwake. The other faces are associated with the lands of [[Chikushi Province|Chikushi]] ([[Shirahiwake]]), [[Hikuni]] ([[Tatehihayahi-toyo-kushibi-nidoriwake]]), and [[Kumaso Province|Kumaso]] ([[Tatehiwake]]).
{{quote|次生筑紫嶋、此嶋亦、身一而有面四、毎面有名、故、筑紫國謂白日別、'''豐國謂豐日別'''、肥國謂建日向日豐久士比泥別自久至泥、以音、熊曾國謂建日別。曾字以音。|古事記}}
Edo-period scholar {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} speculated that these names, including Toyohiwake, were the original names of the regions during the [[Age of the Gods]] and that names like Chikushi or Toyokuni were adopted later under the [[Ritsuryō system]].<ref>{{cite book|和書|author=佐藤蔵太郎|year=1905|title=豊後史蹟考|publisher=甲斐書店|location=大分県}}</ref>
== Shrines == Toyohiwake is worshipped at several shrines across Japan, particularly in Kyushu.
=== Toyohiwake-no-miya === The {{ill|Toyohiwake-no-miya|ja|豊日別宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (豊日別宮) in [[Yukuhashi]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], holds a tradition that dates back to Emperor [[Kinmei]]'s reign in the 6th century. According to its lore, the deity Toyohiwake was associated with the god [[Sarutahiko]], who guided [[Amaterasu]]'s descendant during the celestial descent. Toyohiwake is sometimes equated with Sarutahiko but is understood to have distinct qualities.<ref>{{cite book|和書|editor=佐藤蔵太郎|title=豊後史蹟考|year=1905|publisher=甲斐書店}}</ref>
=== Unzen Shrine === Known locally as {{nihongo|Shimen-gū|四面宮}}, the [[Unzen Shrine]] in Nagasaki Prefecture venerates Toyohiwake and similar four-faced deities. This shrine system spread across the Shimabara Peninsula and is a local center of worship.<ref>[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会ホームページ]</ref>
=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Toyo Province]] * [[Zenpokoenfun]]

{{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Toyohiwake | image = | caption = | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | classification = [[Kunitama]] (Spirit of the Land) | full_name = | alternate_name = {{ill|豊国|ja}}|other_names = | Rank = | role = Spirit of the land ([[Kunitama]]) | burial_site= | father = [[Izanagi]] | mother = [[Izanami]] | siblings = | spouse = | children = | shrines = [[Toyohiwake-no-miya]] and others | blessings = | myths = | related_clans =|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Toyohiwake''' ({{lang|ja|豊日別}}) is a deity featured in the [[Kojiki]] and is associated with the creation of Japan's lands.
== Overview == In the section of the [[Kojiki]] describing the [[Kuniumi|creation of the land]], the deities [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] gave birth to the great islands of Japan, known as the [[Ōyashima]]. The island of [[Kyushu]], like [[Iyo-no-futana-shima]] (modern [[Shikoku]]), is said to have one body but four faces, each with its own name. The second face, corresponding to the land of [[Toyokuni]] (modern [[Bungo Province]]), is called Toyohiwake. The other faces are associated with the lands of [[Chikushi Province|Chikushi]] ([[Shirahiwake]]), [[Hikuni]] ([[Tatehihayahi-toyo-kushibi-nidoriwake]]), and [[Kumaso Province|Kumaso]] ([[Tatehiwake]]).
{{quote|次生筑紫嶋、此嶋亦、身一而有面四、毎面有名、故、筑紫國謂白日別、'''豐國謂豐日別'''、肥國謂建日向日豐久士比泥別自久至泥、以音、熊曾國謂建日別。曾字以音。|古事記}}
Edo-period scholar {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} speculated that these names, including Toyohiwake, were the original names of the regions during the [[Age of the Gods]] and that names like Chikushi or Toyokuni were adopted later under the [[Ritsuryō system]].<ref>{{cite book|和書|author=佐藤蔵太郎|year=1905|title=豊後史蹟考|publisher=甲斐書店|location=大分県}}</ref>
== Shrines == Toyohiwake is worshipped at several shrines across Japan, particularly in Kyushu.
=== Toyohiwake-no-miya === The {{ill|Toyohiwake-no-miya|ja|豊日別宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (豊日別宮) in [[Yukuhashi]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], holds a tradition that dates back to Emperor [[Kinmei]]'s reign in the 6th century. According to its lore, the deity Toyohiwake was associated with the god [[Sarutahiko]], who guided [[Amaterasu]]'s descendant during the celestial descent. Toyohiwake is sometimes equated with Sarutahiko but is understood to have distinct qualities.<ref>{{cite book|和書|editor=佐藤蔵太郎|title=豊後史蹟考|year=1905|publisher=甲斐書店}}</ref>
=== Unzen Shrine === Known locally as {{nihongo|Shimen-gū|四面宮}}, the [[Unzen Shrine]] in Nagasaki Prefecture venerates Toyohiwake and similar four-faced deities. This shrine system spread across the Shimabara Peninsula and is a local center of worship.<ref>[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会ホームページ]</ref>
=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Toyo Province]] * [[Zenpokoenfun]]

=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Toyo Province]] * [[Zenpokoenfun]]
== References == {{reflist}}

<!--Toyohiwake no Mikoto--><!--Toyohiwake no Mikoto--><!--Toyohiwake no Mikoto--><!--Toyohiwake no Mikoto--><!--Toyohiwake no Mikoto--><!--Toyohiwake no Mikoto--><!--Toyohiwake no Mikoto--> [[Category:豊国の人物]]

{{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Toyohiwake | image = | caption = | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | classification = [[Kunitama]] (Spirit of the Land) | full_name = | alternate_name = {{ill|豊国|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/豊国|qq=}}|other_names = | Rank = | role = Spirit of the land ([[Kunitama]]) | burial_site= | father = [[Izanagi]] | mother = [[Izanami]] | siblings = | spouse = | children = | shrines = [[Toyohiwake-no-miya]] and others | blessings = | myths = | related_clans =|qq=draft|qq=draft}} '''Toyohiwake''' ({{lang|ja|豊日別}}) is a deity featured in the [[Kojiki]] and is associated with the creation of Japan's lands.
== Overview == In the section of the [[Kojiki]] describing the [[Kuniumi|creation of the land]], the deities [[Izanagi]] and [[Izanami]] gave birth to the great islands of Japan, known as the [[Ōyashima]]. The island of [[Kyushu]], like [[Iyo-no-futana-shima]] (modern [[Shikoku]]), is said to have one body but four faces, each with its own name. The second face, corresponding to the land of [[Toyokuni]] (modern [[Bungo Province]]), is called Toyohiwake. The other faces are associated with the lands of [[Chikushi Province|Chikushi]] ([[Shirahiwake]]), [[Hikuni]] ([[Tatehihayahi-toyo-kushibi-nidoriwake]]), and [[Kumaso Province|Kumaso]] ([[Tatehiwake]]).
{{quote|次生筑紫嶋、此嶋亦、身一而有面四、毎面有名、故、筑紫國謂白日別、'''豐國謂豐日別'''、肥國謂建日向日豐久士比泥別自久至泥、以音、熊曾國謂建日別。曾字以音。|古事記}}
Edo-period scholar {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|qq=}} speculated that these names, including Toyohiwake, were the original names of the regions during the [[Age of the Gods]] and that names like Chikushi or Toyokuni were adopted later under the [[Ritsuryō system]].<ref>{{cite book|和書|author=佐藤蔵太郎|year=1905|title=豊後史蹟考|publisher=甲斐書店|location=大分県}}</ref>
== Shrines == Toyohiwake is worshipped at several shrines across Japan, particularly in Kyushu.
=== Toyohiwake-no-miya === The {{ill|Toyohiwake-no-miya|ja|豊日別宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohiwake-no-miya|qq=}} (豊日別宮) in [[Yukuhashi]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]], holds a tradition that dates back to Emperor [[Kinmei]]'s reign in the 6th century. According to its lore, the deity Toyohiwake was associated with the god [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], who guided [[Amaterasu]]'s descendant during the celestial descent. Toyohiwake is sometimes equated with Sarutahiko but is understood to have distinct qualities.<ref>{{cite book|和書|editor=佐藤蔵太郎|title=豊後史蹟考|year=1905|publisher=甲斐書店}}</ref>
=== Unzen Shrine === Known locally as {{nihongo|Shimen-gū|四面宮}}, the [[Unzen Shrine]] in Nagasaki Prefecture venerates Toyohiwake and similar four-faced deities. This shrine system spread across the Shimabara Peninsula and is a local center of worship.<ref>[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会ホームページ]</ref>
=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * [[List of Japanese deities]] * [[Toyo Province]] * [[Zenpokoenfun]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会公式ページ]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11633725]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Toyohiwake | image = | caption = | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | classification = [[Kunitama]] (Spirit of the Land) | full_name = | alternate_name = {{ill|豊国|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/豊国|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|other_names = | Rank = | role = Spirit of the land ([[Kunitama]]) | burial_site= | father = [[Izanagi]] | mother = [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] | siblings = | spouse = | children = | shrines = [[Toyohiwake-no-miya]] and others | blessings = | myths = | related_clans =|qq=draft}} '''Toyohiwake''' ({{lang|ja|豊日別}}) is a deity featured in the [[Kojiki]] and is associated with the creation of Japan's lands.
== Overview == In the section of the [[Kojiki]] describing the [[Kuniumi|creation of the land]], the deities [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] gave birth to the great islands of Japan, known as the [[:en:Ōyashima|Ōyashima]]. The island of [[Kyushu]], like [[Iyo-no-futana-shima]] (modern [[Shikoku]]), is said to have one body but four faces, each with its own name. The second face, corresponding to the land of [[:en:Toyokuni|Toyokuni]] (modern [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]), is called Toyohiwake. The other faces are associated with the lands of [[Chikushi Province|Chikushi]] ([[Shirahiwake]]), [[Hikuni]] ([[Tatehihayahi-toyo-kushibi-nidoriwake]]), and [[:en:Kumaso Province|Kumaso]] ([[Tatehiwake]]).
{{quote|次生筑紫嶋、此嶋亦、身一而有面四、毎面有名、故、筑紫國謂白日別、'''豐國謂豐日別'''、肥國謂建日向日豐久士比泥別自久至泥、以音、熊曾國謂建日別。曾字以音。|古事記}}
Edo-period scholar {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|1=Deguchi Nobuyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q62601522}} speculated that these names, including Toyohiwake, were the original names of the regions during the [[Age of the Gods]] and that names like Chikushi or Toyokuni were adopted later under the [[Ritsuryō system]].<ref>{{cite book|和書|author=佐藤蔵太郎|year=1905|title=豊後史蹟考|publisher=甲斐書店|location=大分県}}</ref>
== Shrines == Toyohiwake is worshipped at several shrines across Japan, particularly in Kyushu.
=== Toyohiwake-no-miya === The {{ill|Toyohiwake-no-miya|ja|豊日別宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohiwake-no-miya|1=Toyohi-wakegū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633726}} (豊日別宮) in [[:en:Yukuhashi|Yukuhashi]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], holds a tradition that dates back to Emperor [[:en:Kinmei|Kinmei]]'s reign in the 6th century. According to its lore, the deity Toyohiwake was associated with the god [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], who guided [[Amaterasu]]'s descendant during the celestial descent. Toyohiwake is sometimes equated with Sarutahiko but is understood to have distinct qualities.<ref>{{cite book|和書|editor=佐藤蔵太郎|title=豊後史蹟考|year=1905|publisher=甲斐書店}}</ref>
=== Unzen Shrine === Known locally as {{nihongo|Shimen-gū|四面宮}}, the [[Unzen Shrine]] in Nagasaki Prefecture venerates Toyohiwake and similar four-faced deities. This shrine system spread across the Shimabara Peninsula and is a local center of worship.<ref>[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会ホームページ]</ref>
=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:Toyo Province|Toyo Province]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会公式ページ]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11633725]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Toyohiwake | image = | caption = | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | classification = [[Kunitama]] (Spirit of the Land) | full_name = | alternate_name = {{ill|豊国|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/豊国|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|other_names = | Rank = | role = Spirit of the land ([[Kunitama]]) | burial_site= | father = [[Izanagi]] | mother = [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] | siblings = | spouse = | children = | shrines = [[Toyohiwake-no-miya]] and others | blessings = | myths = | related_clans =|qq=draft}} '''Toyohiwake''' ({{lang|ja|豊日別}}) is a deity featured in the [[Kojiki]] and is associated with the creation of Japan's lands.
== Overview == In the section of the [[Kojiki]] describing the [[Kuniumi|creation of the land]], the deities [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] gave birth to the great islands of Japan, known as the [[:en:Ōyashima|Ōyashima]]. The island of [[Kyushu]], like [[Iyo-no-futana-shima]] (modern [[Shikoku]]), is said to have one body but four faces, each with its own name. The second face, corresponding to the land of [[:en:Toyokuni|Toyokuni]] (modern [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]), is called Toyohiwake. The other faces are associated with the lands of [[Chikushi Province|Chikushi]] ([[Shirahiwake]]), [[Hikuni]] ([[Tatehihayahi-toyo-kushibi-nidoriwake]]), and [[:en:Kumaso Province|Kumaso]] ([[Tatehiwake]]).
{{quote|次生筑紫嶋、此嶋亦、身一而有面四、毎面有名、故、筑紫國謂白日別、'''豐國謂豐日別'''、肥國謂建日向日豐久士比泥別自久至泥、以音、熊曾國謂建日別。曾字以音。|古事記}}
Edo-period scholar {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|1=Deguchi Nobuyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q62601522}} speculated that these names, including Toyohiwake, were the original names of the regions during the [[Age of the Gods]] and that names like Chikushi or Toyokuni were adopted later under the [[Ritsuryō system]].<ref>{{cite book|和書|author=佐藤蔵太郎|year=1905|title=豊後史蹟考|publisher=甲斐書店|location=大分県}}</ref>
== Shrines == Toyohiwake is worshipped at several shrines across Japan, particularly in Kyushu.
=== Toyohiwake-no-miya === The {{ill|Toyohiwake-no-miya|ja|豊日別宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohiwake-no-miya|1=Toyohi-wakegū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633726}} (豊日別宮) in [[:en:Yukuhashi|Yukuhashi]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], holds a tradition that dates back to Emperor [[:en:Kinmei|Kinmei]]'s reign in the 6th century. According to its lore, the deity Toyohiwake was associated with the god [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], who guided [[Amaterasu]]'s descendant during the celestial descent. Toyohiwake is sometimes equated with Sarutahiko but is understood to have distinct qualities.<ref>{{cite book|和書|editor=佐藤蔵太郎|title=豊後史蹟考|year=1905|publisher=甲斐書店}}</ref>
=== Unzen Shrine === Known locally as {{nihongo|Shimen-gū|四面宮}}, the [[Unzen Shrine]] in Nagasaki Prefecture venerates Toyohiwake and similar four-faced deities. This shrine system spread across the Shimabara Peninsula and is a local center of worship.<ref>[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会ホームページ]</ref>
=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:Toyo Province|Toyo Province]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会公式ページ]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11633725]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Toyohiwake | image = | caption = | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | classification = [[Kunitama]] (Spirit of the Land) | full_name = | alternate_name = {{ill|豊国|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/豊国|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|other_names = | Rank = | role = Spirit of the land ([[Kunitama]]) | burial_site= | father = [[Izanagi]] | mother = [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] | siblings = | spouse = | children = | shrines = [[Toyohiwake-no-miya]] and others | blessings = | myths = | related_clans =|qq=draft}} '''Toyohiwake''' ({{lang|ja|豊日別}}) is a deity featured in the [[Kojiki]] and is associated with the creation of Japan's lands.
== Overview == In the section of the [[Kojiki]] describing the [[Kuniumi|creation of the land]], the deities [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] gave birth to the great islands of Japan, known as the [[:en:Ōyashima|Ōyashima]]. The island of [[Kyushu]], like [[Iyo-no-futana-shima]] (modern [[Shikoku]]), is said to have one body but four faces, each with its own name. The second face, corresponding to the land of [[:en:Toyokuni|Toyokuni]] (modern [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]), is called Toyohiwake. The other faces are associated with the lands of [[Chikushi Province|Chikushi]] ([[Shirahiwake]]), [[Hikuni]] ([[Tatehihayahi-toyo-kushibi-nidoriwake]]), and [[:en:Kumaso Province|Kumaso]] ([[Tatehiwake]]).
{{quote|次生筑紫嶋、此嶋亦、身一而有面四、毎面有名、故、筑紫國謂白日別、'''豐國謂豐日別'''、肥國謂建日向日豐久士比泥別自久至泥、以音、熊曾國謂建日別。曾字以音。|古事記}}
Edo-period scholar {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|1=Deguchi Nobuyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q62601522}} speculated that these names, including Toyohiwake, were the original names of the regions during the [[Age of the Gods]] and that names like Chikushi or Toyokuni were adopted later under the [[Ritsuryō system]].<ref>{{cite book|和書|author=佐藤蔵太郎|year=1905|title=豊後史蹟考|publisher=甲斐書店|location=大分県}}</ref>
== Shrines == Toyohiwake is worshipped at several shrines across Japan, particularly in Kyushu.
=== Toyohiwake-no-miya === The {{ill|Toyohiwake-no-miya|ja|豊日別宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohiwake-no-miya|1=Toyohi-wakegū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633726}} (豊日別宮) in [[:en:Yukuhashi|Yukuhashi]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], holds a tradition that dates back to Emperor [[:en:Kinmei|Kinmei]]'s reign in the 6th century. According to its lore, the deity Toyohiwake was associated with the god [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], who guided [[Amaterasu]]'s descendant during the celestial descent. Toyohiwake is sometimes equated with Sarutahiko but is understood to have distinct qualities.<ref>{{cite book|和書|editor=佐藤蔵太郎|title=豊後史蹟考|year=1905|publisher=甲斐書店}}</ref>
=== Unzen Shrine === Known locally as {{nihongo|Shimen-gū|四面宮}}, the [[Unzen Shrine]] in Nagasaki Prefecture venerates Toyohiwake and similar four-faced deities. This shrine system spread across the Shimabara Peninsula and is a local center of worship.<ref>[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会ホームページ]</ref>
=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:Toyo Province|Toyo Province]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会公式ページ]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11633725]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Toyohiwake | image = | caption = | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | classification = [[Kunitama]] (Spirit of the Land) | full_name = | alternate_name = {{ill|豊国|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/豊国|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|other_names = | Rank = | role = Spirit of the land ([[Kunitama]]) | burial_site= | father = [[Izanagi]] | mother = [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] | siblings = | spouse = | children = | shrines = [[Toyohiwake-no-miya]] and others | blessings = | myths = | related_clans =|qq=draft}} '''Toyohiwake''' ({{lang|ja|豊日別}}) is a deity featured in the [[Kojiki]] and is associated with the creation of Japan's lands.
== Overview == In the section of the [[Kojiki]] describing the [[Kuniumi|creation of the land]], the deities [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] gave birth to the great islands of Japan, known as the [[:en:Ōyashima|Ōyashima]]. The island of [[Kyushu]], like [[Iyo-no-futana-shima]] (modern [[Shikoku]]), is said to have one body but four faces, each with its own name. The second face, corresponding to the land of [[:en:Toyokuni|Toyokuni]] (modern [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]), is called Toyohiwake. The other faces are associated with the lands of [[Chikushi Province|Chikushi]] ([[Shirahiwake]]), [[Hikuni]] ([[Tatehihayahi-toyo-kushibi-nidoriwake]]), and [[:en:Kumaso Province|Kumaso]] ([[Tatehiwake]]).
{{quote|次生筑紫嶋、此嶋亦、身一而有面四、毎面有名、故、筑紫國謂白日別、'''豐國謂豐日別'''、肥國謂建日向日豐久士比泥別自久至泥、以音、熊曾國謂建日別。曾字以音。|古事記}}
Edo-period scholar {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|1=Deguchi Nobuyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q62601522}} speculated that these names, including Toyohiwake, were the original names of the regions during the [[Age of the Gods]] and that names like Chikushi or Toyokuni were adopted later under the [[Ritsuryō system]].<ref>{{cite book|和書|author=佐藤蔵太郎|year=1905|title=豊後史蹟考|publisher=甲斐書店|location=大分県}}</ref>
== Shrines == Toyohiwake is worshipped at several shrines across Japan, particularly in Kyushu.
=== Toyohiwake-no-miya === The {{ill|Toyohiwake-no-miya|ja|豊日別宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohiwake-no-miya|1=Toyohi-wakegū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633726}} (豊日別宮) in [[:en:Yukuhashi|Yukuhashi]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], holds a tradition that dates back to Emperor [[:en:Kinmei|Kinmei]]'s reign in the 6th century. According to its lore, the deity Toyohiwake was associated with the god [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], who guided [[Amaterasu]]'s descendant during the celestial descent. Toyohiwake is sometimes equated with Sarutahiko but is understood to have distinct qualities.<ref>{{cite book|和書|editor=佐藤蔵太郎|title=豊後史蹟考|year=1905|publisher=甲斐書店}}</ref>
=== Unzen Shrine === Known locally as {{nihongo|Shimen-gū|四面宮}}, the [[Unzen Shrine]] in Nagasaki Prefecture venerates Toyohiwake and similar four-faced deities. This shrine system spread across the Shimabara Peninsula and is a local center of worship.<ref>[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会ホームページ]</ref>
=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:Toyo Province|Toyo Province]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会公式ページ]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11633725]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Toyohiwake | image = | caption = | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | classification = [[Kunitama]] (Spirit of the Land) | full_name = | alternate_name = {{ill|豊国|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/豊国|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|other_names = | Rank = | role = Spirit of the land ([[Kunitama]]) | burial_site= | father = [[Izanagi]] | mother = [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] | siblings = | spouse = | children = | shrines = [[Toyohiwake-no-miya]] and others | blessings = | myths = | related_clans =|qq=draft}} '''Toyohiwake''' ({{lang|ja|豊日別}}) is a deity featured in the [[Kojiki]] and is associated with the creation of Japan's lands.
== Overview == In the section of the [[Kojiki]] describing the [[Kuniumi|creation of the land]], the deities [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] gave birth to the great islands of Japan, known as the [[:en:Ōyashima|Ōyashima]]. The island of [[Kyushu]], like [[Iyo-no-futana-shima]] (modern [[Shikoku]]), is said to have one body but four faces, each with its own name. The second face, corresponding to the land of [[:en:Toyokuni|Toyokuni]] (modern [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]), is called Toyohiwake. The other faces are associated with the lands of [[Chikushi Province|Chikushi]] ([[Shirahiwake]]), [[Hikuni]] ([[Tatehihayahi-toyo-kushibi-nidoriwake]]), and [[:en:Kumaso Province|Kumaso]] ([[Tatehiwake]]).
{{quote|次生筑紫嶋、此嶋亦、身一而有面四、毎面有名、故、筑紫國謂白日別、'''豐國謂豐日別'''、肥國謂建日向日豐久士比泥別自久至泥、以音、熊曾國謂建日別。曾字以音。|古事記}}
Edo-period scholar {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|1=Deguchi Nobuyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q62601522}} speculated that these names, including Toyohiwake, were the original names of the regions during the [[Age of the Gods]] and that names like Chikushi or Toyokuni were adopted later under the [[Ritsuryō system]].<ref>{{cite book|和書|author=佐藤蔵太郎|year=1905|title=豊後史蹟考|publisher=甲斐書店|location=大分県}}</ref>
== Shrines == Toyohiwake is worshipped at several shrines across Japan, particularly in Kyushu.
=== Toyohiwake-no-miya === The {{ill|Toyohiwake-no-miya|ja|豊日別宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohiwake-no-miya|1=Toyohi-wakegū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633726}} (豊日別宮) in [[:en:Yukuhashi|Yukuhashi]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], holds a tradition that dates back to Emperor [[:en:Kinmei|Kinmei]]'s reign in the 6th century. According to its lore, the deity Toyohiwake was associated with the god [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], who guided [[Amaterasu]]'s descendant during the celestial descent. Toyohiwake is sometimes equated with Sarutahiko but is understood to have distinct qualities.<ref>{{cite book|和書|editor=佐藤蔵太郎|title=豊後史蹟考|year=1905|publisher=甲斐書店}}</ref>
=== Unzen Shrine === Known locally as {{nihongo|Shimen-gū|四面宮}}, the [[Unzen Shrine]] in Nagasaki Prefecture venerates Toyohiwake and similar four-faced deities. This shrine system spread across the Shimabara Peninsula and is a local center of worship.<ref>[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会ホームページ]</ref>
=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:Toyo Province|Toyo Province]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会公式ページ]

}}
[[zh:%E8%B1%90%E6%97%A5%E5%88%A5]] [[ja:豊日別]]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Pages with conflicting qids]] to [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633725]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Toyohiwake | image = | caption = | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | classification = [[Kunitama]] (Spirit of the Land) | full_name = | alternate_name = {{ill|豊国|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/豊国|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|other_names = | Rank = | role = Spirit of the land ([[Kunitama]]) | burial_site= | father = [[Izanagi]] | mother = [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] | siblings = | spouse = | children = | shrines = [[Toyohiwake-no-miya]] and others | blessings = | myths = | related_clans =|qq=draft}} '''Toyohiwake''' ({{lang|ja|豊日別}}) is a deity featured in the [[Kojiki]] and is associated with the creation of Japan's lands.
== Overview == In the section of the [[Kojiki]] describing the [[Kuniumi|creation of the land]], the deities [[Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] gave birth to the great islands of Japan, known as the [[:en:Ōyashima|Ōyashima]]. The island of [[Kyushu]], like [[Iyo-no-futana-shima]] (modern [[Shikoku]]), is said to have one body but four faces, each with its own name. The second face, corresponding to the land of [[:en:Toyokuni|Toyokuni]] (modern [[:en:Bungo Province|Bungo Province]]), is called Toyohiwake. The other faces are associated with the lands of [[Chikushi Province|Chikushi]] ([[Shirahiwake]]), [[Hikuni]] ([[Tatehihayahi-toyo-kushibi-nidoriwake]]), and [[:en:Kumaso Province|Kumaso]] ([[Tatehiwake]]).
{{quote|次生筑紫嶋、此嶋亦、身一而有面四、毎面有名、故、筑紫國謂白日別、'''豐國謂豐日別'''、肥國謂建日向日豐久士比泥別自久至泥、以音、熊曾國謂建日別。曾字以音。|古事記}}
Edo-period scholar {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|1=Deguchi Nobuyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q62601522}} speculated that these names, including Toyohiwake, were the original names of the regions during the [[Age of the Gods]] and that names like Chikushi or Toyokuni were adopted later under the [[Ritsuryō system]].<ref>{{cite book|和書|author=佐藤蔵太郎|year=1905|title=豊後史蹟考|publisher=甲斐書店|location=大分県}}</ref>
== Shrines == Toyohiwake is worshipped at several shrines across Japan, particularly in Kyushu.
=== Toyohiwake-no-miya === The {{ill|Toyohiwake-no-miya|ja|豊日別宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohiwake-no-miya|1=Toyohi-wakegū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633726}} (豊日別宮) in [[:en:Yukuhashi|Yukuhashi]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], holds a tradition that dates back to Emperor [[:en:Kinmei|Kinmei]]'s reign in the 6th century. According to its lore, the deity Toyohiwake was associated with the god [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], who guided [[Amaterasu]]'s descendant during the celestial descent. Toyohiwake is sometimes equated with Sarutahiko but is understood to have distinct qualities.<ref>{{cite book|和書|editor=佐藤蔵太郎|title=豊後史蹟考|year=1905|publisher=甲斐書店}}</ref>
=== Unzen Shrine === Known locally as {{nihongo|Shimen-gū|四面宮}}, the [[Unzen Shrine]] in Nagasaki Prefecture venerates Toyohiwake and similar four-faced deities. This shrine system spread across the Shimabara Peninsula and is a local center of worship.<ref>[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会ホームページ]</ref>
=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * [[:en:List of Japanese deities|List of Japanese deities]] * [[:en:Toyo Province|Toyo Province]] * [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会公式ページ]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:Wikidata has English label]]" to "[[Category:qq]]"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633725]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Toyohiwake | image = | caption = | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | classification = [[Kunitama]] (Spirit of the Land) | full_name = | alternate_name = {{ill|豊国|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/豊国|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|other_names = | Rank = | role = Spirit of the land ([[Kunitama]]) | burial_site= | father = [[Izanagi]] | mother = {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = | shrines = [[Toyohiwake-no-miya]] and others | blessings = | myths = | related_clans =|qq=draft}} '''Toyohiwake''' ({{lang|ja|豊日別}}) is a deity featured in the [[Kojiki]] and is associated with the creation of Japan's lands.
== Overview == In the section of the [[Kojiki]] describing the [[Kuniumi|creation of the land]], the deities [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} gave birth to the great islands of Japan, known as the [[:en:Ōyashima|Ōyashima]]. The island of [[Kyushu]], like [[Iyo-no-futana-shima]] (modern [[Shikoku]]), is said to have one body but four faces, each with its own name. The second face, corresponding to the land of {{ill|Toyokuni|en|Toyokuni|WD=Q1305760|lt=Toyokuni}} (modern {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province}}), is called Toyohiwake. The other faces are associated with the lands of [[Chikushi Province|Chikushi]] ([[Shirahiwake]]), [[Hikuni]] ([[Tatehihayahi-toyo-kushibi-nidoriwake]]), and {{ill|Kumaso Province|en|Kumaso Province|WD=Q6443650|lt=Kumaso}} ([[Tatehiwake]]).
{{quote|次生筑紫嶋、此嶋亦、身一而有面四、毎面有名、故、筑紫國謂白日別、'''豐國謂豐日別'''、肥國謂建日向日豐久士比泥別自久至泥、以音、熊曾國謂建日別。曾字以音。|古事記}}
Edo-period scholar {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|1=Deguchi Nobuyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q62601522}} speculated that these names, including Toyohiwake, were the original names of the regions during the [[Age of the Gods]] and that names like Chikushi or Toyokuni were adopted later under the [[Ritsuryō system]].<ref>{{cite book|和書|author=佐藤蔵太郎|year=1905|title=豊後史蹟考|publisher=甲斐書店|location=大分県}}</ref>
== Shrines == Toyohiwake is worshipped at several shrines across Japan, particularly in Kyushu.
=== Toyohiwake-no-miya === The {{ill|Toyohiwake-no-miya|ja|豊日別宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohiwake-no-miya|1=Toyohi-wakegū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633726}} (豊日別宮) in [[:en:Yukuhashi|Yukuhashi]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, holds a tradition that dates back to Emperor [[:en:Kinmei|Kinmei]]'s reign in the 6th century. According to its lore, the deity Toyohiwake was associated with the god [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], who guided [[Amaterasu]]'s descendant during the celestial descent. Toyohiwake is sometimes equated with Sarutahiko but is understood to have distinct qualities.<ref>{{cite book|和書|editor=佐藤蔵太郎|title=豊後史蹟考|year=1905|publisher=甲斐書店}}</ref>
=== Unzen Shrine === Known locally as {{nihongo|Shimen-gū|四面宮}}, the [[Unzen Shrine]] in Nagasaki Prefecture venerates Toyohiwake and similar four-faced deities. This shrine system spread across the Shimabara Peninsula and is a local center of worship.<ref>[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会ホームページ]</ref>
=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Toyo Province|en|Toyo Province|WD=Q1205043|lt=Toyo Province}} * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会公式ページ]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] {{Infobox deity}} {{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Toyohiwake | image = | caption = | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | classification = [[Kunitama]] (Spirit of the Land) | full_name = | alternate_name = {{ill|豊国|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/豊国|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|other_names = | Rank = | role = Spirit of the land ([[Kunitama]]) | burial_site= | father = [[Izanagi]] | mother = {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = | shrines = [[Toyohiwake-no-miya]] and others | blessings = | myths = | related_clans =|qq=draft}} '''Toyohiwake''' ({{lang|ja|豊日別}}) is a deity featured in the [[Kojiki]] and is associated with the creation of Japan's lands.
== Overview == In the section of the [[Kojiki]] describing the [[Kuniumi|creation of the land]], the deities [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} gave birth to the great islands of Japan, known as the [[:en:Ōyashima|Ōyashima]]. The island of [[Kyushu]], like [[Iyo-no-futana-shima]] (modern [[Shikoku]]), is said to have one body but four faces, each with its own name. The second face, corresponding to the land of {{ill|Toyokuni|en|Toyokuni|WD=Q1305760|lt=Toyokuni|1=Toyokuni}} (modern {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}), is called Toyohiwake. The other faces are associated with the lands of [[Chikushi Province|Chikushi]] ([[Shirahiwake]]), [[Hikuni]] ([[Tatehihayahi-toyo-kushibi-nidoriwake]]), and {{ill|Kumaso Province|en|Kumaso Province|WD=Q6443650|lt=Kumaso|1=Kumaso Province}} ([[Tatehiwake]]).
{{quote|次生筑紫嶋、此嶋亦、身一而有面四、毎面有名、故、筑紫國謂白日別、'''豐國謂豐日別'''、肥國謂建日向日豐久士比泥別自久至泥、以音、熊曾國謂建日別。曾字以音。|古事記}}
Edo-period scholar {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|1=Deguchi Nobuyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q62601522}} speculated that these names, including Toyohiwake, were the original names of the regions during the [[Age of the Gods]] and that names like Chikushi or Toyokuni were adopted later under the [[Ritsuryō system]].<ref>{{cite book|和書|author=佐藤蔵太郎|year=1905|title=豊後史蹟考|publisher=甲斐書店|location=大分県}}</ref>
== Shrines == Toyohiwake is worshipped at several shrines across Japan, particularly in Kyushu.
=== Toyohiwake-no-miya === The {{ill|Toyohiwake-no-miya|ja|豊日別宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohiwake-no-miya|1=Toyohi-wakegū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633726}} (豊日別宮) in [[:en:Yukuhashi|Yukuhashi]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, holds a tradition that dates back to Emperor [[:en:Kinmei|Kinmei]]'s reign in the 6th century. According to its lore, the deity Toyohiwake was associated with the god [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], who guided [[Amaterasu]]'s descendant during the celestial descent. Toyohiwake is sometimes equated with Sarutahiko but is understood to have distinct qualities.<ref>{{cite book|和書|editor=佐藤蔵太郎|title=豊後史蹟考|year=1905|publisher=甲斐書店}}</ref>
=== Unzen Shrine === Known locally as {{nihongo|Shimen-gū|四面宮}}, the [[Unzen Shrine]] in Nagasaki Prefecture venerates Toyohiwake and similar four-faced deities. This shrine system spread across the Shimabara Peninsula and is a local center of worship.<ref>[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会ホームページ]</ref>
=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities|1=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Toyo Province|en|Toyo Province|WD=Q1205043|lt=Toyo Province|1=Toyo Province}} * {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会公式ページ]

=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities|1=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Toyo Province|en|Toyo Province|WD=Q1205043|lt=Toyo Province|1=Toyo Province}} * {{ill|Zenpōkōenfun|en|Zenpōkōenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}
== References == {{reflist}}
== External Links == * [https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会公式ページ]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:豊日別]][[zh:豐日別]] {{Infobox deity}} {{Infobox Shinto deity | name = Toyohiwake | image = | caption = | generation = | predecessor = | successor = | classification = [[Kunitama]] (Spirit of the Land) | full_name = | alternate_name = {{ill|豊国|ja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/豊国|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}|other_names = | Rank = | role = Spirit of the land ([[Kunitama]]) | burial_site= | father = [[Izanagi]] | mother = {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} | siblings = | spouse = | children = | shrines = [[Toyohiwake-no-miya]] and others | blessings = | myths = | related_clans =|qq=draft}} '''Toyohiwake''' ({{lang|ja|豊日別}}) is a deity featured in the [[Kojiki]] and is associated with the creation of Japan's lands.
== Overview == In the section of the [[Kojiki]] describing the [[Kuniumi|creation of the land]], the deities [[Izanagi]] and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} gave birth to the great islands of Japan, known as the [[:en:Ōyashima|Ōyashima]]. The island of [[Kyushu]], like [[Iyo-no-futana-shima]] (modern [[Shikoku]]), is said to have one body but four faces, each with its own name. The second face, corresponding to the land of {{ill|Toyokuni|en|Toyokuni|WD=Q1305760|lt=Toyokuni|1=Toyokuni}} (modern {{ill|Bungo Province|en|Bungo Province|WD=Q864148|lt=Bungo Province|1=Bungo Province}}), is called Toyohiwake. The other faces are associated with the lands of [[Chikushi Province|Chikushi]] ([[Shirahiwake]]), [[Hikuni]] ([[Tatehihayahi-toyo-kushibi-nidoriwake]]), and {{ill|Kumaso Province|en|Kumaso Province|WD=Q6443650|lt=Kumaso|1=Kumaso Province}} ([[Tatehiwake]]).
{{quote|次生筑紫嶋、此嶋亦、身一而有面四、毎面有名、故、筑紫國謂白日別、'''豐國謂豐日別'''、肥國謂建日向日豐久士比泥別自久至泥、以音、熊曾國謂建日別。曾字以音。|古事記}}
Edo-period scholar {{ill|Deguchi Nobuyoshi|ja|出口延佳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Deguchi Nobuyoshi|1=Deguchi Nobuyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q62601522}} speculated that these names, including Toyohiwake, were the original names of the regions during the [[Age of the Gods]] and that names like Chikushi or Toyokuni were adopted later under the [[Ritsuryō system]].<ref>{{cite book|和書|author=佐藤蔵太郎|year=1905|title=豊後史蹟考|publisher=甲斐書店|location=大分県}}</ref>
== Shrines == Toyohiwake is worshipped at several shrines across Japan, particularly in Kyushu.
=== Toyohiwake-no-miya === The {{ill|Toyohiwake-no-miya|ja|豊日別宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyohiwake-no-miya|1=Toyohi-wakegū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633726}} (豊日別宮) in [[:en:Yukuhashi|Yukuhashi]], {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, holds a tradition that dates back to Emperor [[:en:Kinmei|Kinmei]]'s reign in the 6th century. According to its lore, the deity Toyohiwake was associated with the god [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], who guided [[Amaterasu]]'s descendant during the celestial descent. Toyohiwake is sometimes equated with Sarutahiko but is understood to have distinct qualities.<ref>{{cite book|和書|editor=佐藤蔵太郎|title=豊後史蹟考|year=1905|publisher=甲斐書店}}</ref>
=== Unzen Shrine === Known locally as {{nihongo|Shimen-gū|四面宮}}, the [[Unzen Shrine]] in Nagasaki Prefecture venerates Toyohiwake and similar four-faced deities. This shrine system spread across the Shimabara Peninsula and is a local center of worship.<ref>[https://shimengu-jinja.jp/ 四面宮会ホームページ]</ref>
=== Takasumi Shrine === Located at the base of Mount [[Hikosan]] in [[:en:Oita Prefecture|Oita Prefecture]], Takasumi Shrine venerates Toyohiwake as a guardian of the region. Known also as the [[Hikosan]] deity, it holds connections to mountain worship traditions.
=== Others === Toyohiwake is also honored at shrines like [[Itouzu Hachiman Shrine]] and [[Yami-no-hama Shrine]]. These shrines emphasize its role as a protector of the land and its people.
== Related Concepts == Toyohiwake is often associated with other [[Kunitama]] (land spirits) and has connections to [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko]], [[Takamimusubi|Takami-Musubi]], and the mountain deities of northern Kyushu. == See Also == * [[Kuniumi]] * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|List of Japanese deities|WD=Q3240847|lt=List of Japanese deities|1=List of Japanese deities}} * {{ill|Toyo Province|en|Toyo Province|WD=Q1205043|lt=Toyo Province|1=Toyo Province}} * {{ill|Zenpō-kōen-fun|en|Zenpō-kōen-fun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun}}
